metadata.title: The Continuum Was the Bug: Exact Structural Replacements for Three Continuum Pathologies
metadata.description:
Smithian Fold Theory derives a positive finite physical substrate instead of assuming a continuum and later discretising it. From one machine-checked self-proven theorem, zero axioms and zero fitted parameters, the fold forces exact counts, a terminal spatial floor and finite closure.

This rebuilt edition presents three current structural results without carrying forward obsolete inferences. The Riemann functional reflection has the unique real fixed axis 1/2, with off-axis values explicitly paired. The strong sector has positive gap share 1/3 and self-coupling 2/3 in a closed period-two fold cycle that partitions the One. The fluid sector has exact floor 1/32 and vorticity cap 32; a complete 512-cell material run moves the global maximum 800 to 32 while preserving total density 150 to 150.

The same lattice reproduces the centred curvature of x squared exactly at registered spacings, and its depth-five binary ladder closes the One exactly. The paper argues that continuum mathematics can retain its valid relations without being granted authority as the physical primitive.

The Riemann fixed-axis theorem is stated exactly: symmetry alone does not exclude reflected off-axis zero pairs, so the next constructive objective is machine-checking that additional relation. This is forward derivation work, not evidence of a theoretical wall.

The complete synchronized release executes 326 suites and 2,002 exact checks with zero failures, with 326 generated-C certificates identical to source. Open source: Smithian Fold Theory of Everything. Scientific author and publication authority: Maria Smith, Ernos Labs.
metadata.notes: Authoritative rebuilt edition. The finished PDF is the only PDF and primary preview; the Markdown source follows.
